reforms.description: The Belgian government has introduced tax reductions for researchers in enterprises that cooperate with approved research centres and public research institutions, halving the withholding tax on their salaries and promoting collaboration and innovation.
reforms.component_name: Tax Reduction for Private Sector Researchers
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing collaboration between private and public sectors, the reform enhances innovation capacity and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax reductions have been in effect since January 2006.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly encourages private sector engagement in research but does not represent a structural overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: Other tax cuts to stimulate the economy include a halving of withholding tax on the salaries of researchers in enterprises, which have concluded a co-operation agreement with an approved research centre.

reforms.description: The Belgian government has launched several support programmes aimed at correcting market failures in financing innovative SMEs, providing necessary capital to stimulate innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By addressing financing gaps, the reform enhances the capacity of SMEs to innovate and grow, contributing positively to the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The support programmes are currently operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in support for SMEs, addressing critical market failures.
reforms.source_quote: The Belgian governments have launched a number of support programmes in an attempt to correct for this market failure.

reforms.description: The 2004 launch of the Euronext Free Market aimed to attract SMEs in the growth phase by minimizing listing requirements, thus facilitating access to capital markets for smaller firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By easing access to capital markets, the reform supports the growth and innovation potential of SMEs.
reforms.status_evidence: The Euronext Free Market has been operational since its launch in 2004.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly improves access to capital for SMEs but does not represent a major overhaul of the financial system.
reforms.source_quote: One measure to address this market failure was the 2004 Euronext launch of the Free Market – a stock market segment aimed at attracting SMEs in the growth phase, by minimising the listing requirements.

reforms.description: The 2006 launch of Alternext provided a lighter regulatory framework for small and mid-sized companies, ensuring simplified access to capital markets while promoting investor protection.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By simplifying regulations, the reform enhances access to capital for SMEs, fostering innovation and growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Alternext has been operational since its launch in 2006.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly improves the regulatory environment for SMEs but does not represent a major systemic change.
reforms.source_quote: This was followed up by the 2006 launch of Alternext which fills the gap between the Euronext Free market and First Market with respect to reporting obligations.
